frameworks.name: transformer networks
frameworks.one_line: Attention-based sequence models compared head-to-head with humans on rule-learning.

frameworks.name: in-context vs in-weights learning
frameworks.one_line: Dichotomy between generalizing a rule and recalling stored exemplars.

frameworks.name: curriculum learning
frameworks.one_line: Dynamic schedules emphasizing diverse examples early — helps humans, not transformers.

core_question: Do humans and transformer networks show the same sensitivity to training-data distribution when learning rules?
key_assumptions: rule-learning can be probed via sequence query responses
in-context and in-weights strategies are dissociable measurable modes
training-distribution diversity and redundancy are independently manipulable
key_findings: humans and transformers respond very similarly to distributional manipulations
redundancy drives in-weights learning while diversity drives in-context learning
balanced composite distributions enable both strategies in tandem
humans benefit from diverse-early curricula but transformers do not
interpretations: same data-distributional properties promote learning in humans and transformers
curriculum sensitivity is a human-specific divergence from transformer learning
